inline_comments.message: I don't think this will work, because the configuration will not be in Consul.   The Helm charts that deploy SCH supply configuration via a local file.   See https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=oom.git;a=blob;f=kubernetes/dcaegen2/charts/dcae-servicechange-handler/templates/deployment.yaml;h=44ebc42412bf0d3a09579202037f1dcd2fae6dda;hb=HEAD  lines 61-66.   See also https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=oom.git;a=blob;f=kubernetes/dcaegen2/charts/dcae-servicechange-handler/resources/config/config.json;h=7ddc800817c2ec07ded6fe5208c3513645ea99be;hb=HEAD, which supplies the configuration file.  I suspect this may need to be changed as well, as I see an http URL for inventory as one of the configuration elements.

The use of consul as source of configuration was there before as the cmd default.  I just didn't change it.  I have changes lined up on the oom side that overrides this default with the local file approach and I also have changes to the inventory URL to use https.

I can change this to the local file path in the oom chart for SCH.  I can also alternatively just throw an error saying the SCH_ARGS variable hasn't been set.  I think I'd rather do that..

In the Dublin release, we started deploying SCH using Helm.  The Helm chart specified the startup command as follows:
          command: ["java"]
          args:
            - "-jar"           
            - "/opt/servicechange-handler.jar"
            - "prod"
            - "/opt/config.json"
If we don't change this chart, we won't invoke the sch.sh script. But if we do change the chart to run "sch.sh", the SCH will go looking in Consul for a configuration that isn't there.

I am asking for this line to read SCH_ARGS="prod /opt/config.json".
Or, if you really intend to go back to using Consul as the source of config information, I'd like to know how the information will be put there.
